svg.aria-label: Why incremental? — CocoIndex's four production benefits in one illustration. Sub-second fresh: the incremental engine propagates source changes to the target in under a second, so agents see the world as it is, not as it was yesterday. 10× cheaper at scale: only the Δ is recomputed on every change — you skip the other 99.9% of your corpus and pay a fraction of the compute, embedding, and LLM bill. Explainable by default: every vector, row, or graph node in the target traces back to its exact source byte — end-to-end lineage for debuggable, auditable, regulator-friendly AI pipelines. Production-grade: Rust core with retries, exponential back-off, dead-letter queues, and no-data-loss guarantees — production-ready for long-horizon AI agents. Keywords: incremental indexing, Δ-only reprocessing, low-latency RAG, sub-second freshness, cost-efficient embeddings, data lineage, retrieval-augmented generation, Rust core, retries, back-off, dead letters, no data loss, long-horizon agents.
svg.title: Why incremental? · Sub-second fresh · 10× cheaper · Explainable · Production-grade (light theme)
svg.desc: Light-theme version of the four-panel why-incremental hero. Four benefits in one illustration — a stopwatch (emerald), a corpus block with a pulsing Δ column (amber), a lineage thread from source byte to target vector (coral), and a shield stamped with the Rust coin (violet). Recolored for white GitHub READMEs — every token clears WCAG AA on #ffffff.

g.text: Agents see the world as it is,
Run only the Δ — skip the other

svg.text: Declare once. Stay live forever. Only the Δ runs.
